claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions. Let me start by gathering some basic information:

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex, he's 8 years old, and he lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand a bit more about your circumstances.

Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, do you provide for his day-to-day care and needs (such as food, clothing, and housing)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm fully responsible for Alex. I take care of all his day-to-day needs - his food, clothes, housing, everything really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Let me ask you another important question:

Are you currently living in the UK, and is Alex also living in the UK with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK. Alex lives here with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I have a few more questions to complete the assessment. 

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Let me just read through the official guidance to ensure I give you an accurate assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, I'm now ready to give you my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- You are responsible for him and provide all his day-to-day care and needs
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You meet all the requirements under UK Child Benefit rules. You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit for Alex.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. You have confirmed that you are responsible for Alex, providing for his day-to-day care and needs including food, clothes, and housing, and he lives with you. Both you and Alex live in the UK. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the official guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you're usually considered responsible if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is fully responsible for their 8-year-old son Alex, who lives with them in the UK. The claimant provides all day-to-day care and needs, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility requirements under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.
